name: Aramaic
notes: STRUCTURAL CONTACT / ANCESTRY STUB (not a modelled G2P target). Aramaic is a Northwest (Central) Semitic language, for centuries the lingua franca of the Near East and the principal adstrate/substrate of Hebrew, of the Mesopotamian and Levantine Arabic dialects, and a co-liturgical language. It exists here only so specs referencing it as a contact node have something to resolve to; the grapheme set is a placeholder and no phonology is claimed (gn.json stub pattern).

sources.author: Gzella, H.
sources.title: A Cultural History of Aramaic
sources.notes: Reference for Aramaic and its contact history (contact node only).
